flashcards.back: A solution is Pareto optimal if no objective can be improved without worsening at least one other objective.
The set of all Pareto optimal solutions representing the optimal trade-offs between conflicting objectives.

flashcards.front: Name two classical methods for multi-objective optimization.
flashcards.back: Weighted sum method and ε-constraint method.

flashcards.front: What is the main advantage of evolutionary algorithms in MOO?
flashcards.back: They can approximate the entire Pareto front in a single run.

flashcards.front: What does NSGA-II stand for and what are its key components?
flashcards.back: Non-dominated Sorting Genetic Algorithm II. It uses non-dominated sorting and crowding distance to maintain diversity.
